source.title: Consequences of commitment to and disengagement from incentives

confidence_rationale: Canonical theory of the disengagement trigger: it is obstruction/non-attainment of a COMMITTED incentive, not an evidence tally -- supports the never-from-accumulated-hopelessness constraint. But the trigger expresses as a slow, stereotyped depression-like PHASE that does the disengaging, not a one-shot appraisal -- so it qualifies the 'one-shot flag' design. Foundational and central; the one-shot mapping is the uncertain part.
failure_signatures: Too late / stuck in the invigoration phase: persistent value-inflation and effort escalation toward an unreachable incentive (perseverative striving; the current concern never offsets).
Too early: premature collapse of commitment before genuine obstruction -> under-persistence and abandonment of still-attainable goals.
Cycle arrest in the depression phase: concern released from the old goal but no reengagement -> clinical depression.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Commitment to an incentive creates a 'current concern' (onset = commitment, offset = consummation or disengagement) that biases cognition until released; obstruction of a committed incentive triggers a stereotyped cycle -- invigoration, then aggression/protest, then a functional depression-like phase that actively dissolves the commitment, then recovery/redirection.
mapping.ree_translation: Directly informs ARC-078's C3 abandon-path: the biological disengagement trigger is an appraisal of obstruction of a COMMITTED goal, not accumulated-hopelessness evidence (consistent with the Bouton constraint). But Klinger models the trigger as an extended affective phase that performs the disengagement -- so a REE abandon trigger should open a brief disengagement STATE, not instantaneously delete the bank entry.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The depression phase is a slow biological process, not an instantaneous switch; collapsing it into a single-timestep abandon flag discards the very stage Klinger says does the disengagement work. Also a 1975 integrative theory, not a direct neural mechanism.
mapping.source_context: Integrative motivational theory synthesizing animal frustration and human incentive/motivation literature; no neural localization.

confidence_components.notes: Canonical and theory-central (high source quality for a theoretical anchor). Mapping fidelity moderate: the trigger-as-appraisal-of-committed-obstruction maps cleanly; the trigger-as-phase qualifies the one-shot design. Transfer risk moderate (1975 theory -> artificial bank).
